result.stdout:
USAGE:
    torque-cli [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli bp get MyBp
    torque-cli bp list
    torque-cli env start demo --duration=100 --name=MyDemoEnv
    torque-cli env bulkstart <CSV file path>>
    torque-cli env get

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help       Prints help information   
    -v, --version    Prints version information

COMMANDS:
    blueprint      Get, List, Validate blueprints              
    environment    Start, End, View Torque environments        
    config         List, Add and Modify user profiles          
    agent          List, associate agents                      
    space          Create, delete spaces, connect repo to space
    eac            Handle environment-as-code actions

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
List, associate agents

USAGE:
    torque-cli agent [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli agent list mySpace
    torque-cli agent associate myAgent mySpace --ns demo --sa mySA

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information

COMMANDS:
    list <SPACE-NAME>                      List agents in Space      
    associate <AGENT-NAME> <SPACE-NAME>    Associate Agent with Space

commands.payload:
DESCRIPTION:
List, associate agents

USAGE:
    torque-cli agent [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli agent list mySpace
    torque-cli agent associate myAgent mySpace --ns demo --sa mySA

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information

COMMANDS:
    list <SPACE-NAME>                      List agents in Space      
    associate <AGENT-NAME> <SPACE-NAME>    Associate Agent with Space

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Associate Agent with Space

USAGE:
    torque-cli agent associate <AGENT-NAME> <SPACE-NAME> [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli agent associate myAgent mySpace --ns demo --sa mySA

ARGUMENTS:
    <AGENT-NAME>    The name of agent to associate with a space
    <SPACE-NAME>    The space name                             

OPTIONS:
                         DEFAULT                                                
    -h, --help                      Prints help information                     
    -t, --token                     The user long token                         
    -p, --profile                   The user profile name                       
        --type <TYPE>    K8S        The type of agent. Must be 'K8S' or 'AKS'   
        --ns <VALUE>                The default namespace where K8s resources   
                                    would be deployed if no other namespace is  
                                    specified in the blueprint                  
        --sa <VALUE>                The default permissions to launch           
                                    environments in this space

commands.payload:
DESCRIPTION:
Associate Agent with Space

ARGUMENTS:
    <AGENT-NAME>    The name of agent to associate with a space
    <SPACE-NAME>    The space name                             

OPTIONS:
                         DEFAULT                                                
    -h, --help                      Prints help information                     
    -t, --token                     The user long token                         
    -p, --profile                   The user profile name                       
        --type <TYPE>    K8S        The type of agent. Must be 'K8S' or 'AKS'   
        --ns <VALUE>                The default namespace where K8s resources   
                                    would be deployed if no other namespace is  
                                    specified in the blueprint                  
        --sa <VALUE>                The default permissions to launch           
                                    environments in this space

DESCRIPTION:
Get, List, Validate blueprints.

USAGE:
    torque-cli blueprint [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli bp get MyBp
    torque-cli bp list

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information

COMMANDS:
    get <BLUEPRINT-NAME>          Get blueprint by Name        
    list                          List blueprints              
    validate <BLUEPRINT-FILE>     Validate blueprint           
    publish <BLUEPRINT-NAME>      Publish blueprint to catalog 
    unpublish <BLUEPRINT-NAME>    Remove blueprint from catalog

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
List, Add and Modify user profiles

USAGE:
    torque-cli config [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli config list
    torque-cli config remove myprofile
    torque-cli config activate myprofile

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information

COMMANDS:
    list                       List all profiles       
    add                        Add user profile        
    update                     Update user profile     
    remove <PROFILE-NAME>      Remove specified profile
    activate <PROFILE-NAME>    Set active profile

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Handle environment-as-code actions.

USAGE:
    torque-cli eac [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli eac list
    torque-cli eac export qwdj4jr9smf
    torque-cli eac plan qwdj4jr9smf
    torque-cli eac plan qwdj4jr9smf -g myGrain

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information

COMMANDS:
    list                       List all current Git-managed environments and    
                               their status                                     
    export <ENVIRONMENT-ID>    Generates an environment YAML file from an       
                               existing environment                             
    plan <ENVIRONMENT-ID>      Runs a plan against an environment and returns   
                               the results

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Generates an environment YAML file from an existing environment.

USAGE:
    torque-cli eac export <ENVIRONMENT-ID> [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli eac export qwdj4jr9smf

ARGUMENTS:
    <ENVIRONMENT-ID>    The environment id for plan

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help       Prints help information                                    
    -t, --token      The user long token                                        
    -p, --profile    The user profile name                                      
    -s, --space      The space name                                             
    -r, --repo       The repository name to use with command                    
    -o, --output     The file name to generate, defaults to the environment's   
                     name

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Runs a plan against an environment and returns the results.

USAGE:
    torque-cli eac plan <ENVIRONMENT-ID> [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli eac plan qwdj4jr9smf
    torque-cli eac plan qwdj4jr9smf -g myGrain

ARGUMENTS:
    <ENVIRONMENT-ID>    The environment id for plan

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help       Prints help information                
    -t, --token      The user long token                    
    -p, --profile    The user profile name                  
    -s, --space      The space name                         
    -r, --repo       The repository name to use with command
    -g, --grain      Return the plan for the specified grain

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Start, End, View Torque environments.

USAGE:
    torque-cli environment [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli env start demo --duration=100 --name=MyDemoEnv
    torque-cli env bulkstart <CSV file path>>
    torque-cli env get
    torque-cli env end qwdj4jr9smf
    torque-cli env list --show-ended

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information

COMMANDS:
    start <BLUEPRINT-NAME>       Start Environment        
    bulkstart <CSV file path>    Start Environment        
    get <ENVIRONMENT-ID>         Get Environment Details  
    end <ENVIRONMENT-ID>         End Torque Environment   
    list                         List Torque Environment  
    extend <ENVIRONMENT-ID>      Extend Torque Environment

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Extend Torque Environment

USAGE:
    torque-cli environment extend <ENVIRONMENT-ID> [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli env extend qwdj4jr9smf --duration 120

ARGUMENTS:
    <ENVIRONMENT-ID>    The environment id to extend

OPTIONS:
                                DEFAULT                                         
    -h, --help                             Prints help information              
    -t, --token                            The user long token                  
    -p, --profile                          The user profile name                
    -s, --space                            The space name                       
    -r, --repo                             The repository name to use with      
                                           command                              
    -d, --duration <MINUTES>    120        The Environment will automatically   
                                           de-provision at the end of the       
                                           provided duration (minutes)

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
List Torque Environment

USAGE:
    torque-cli environment list [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli env list --show-ended

OPTIONS:
                            DEFAULT                                             
    -h, --help                         Prints help information                  
    -t, --token                        The user long token                      
    -p, --profile                      The user profile name                    
    -s, --space                        The space name                           
    -r, --repo                         The repository name to use with command  
        --detail                       Show detailed output in a json format    
        --show-ended                   Show ended environments                  
        --count <N>         20         Amount of environment to be retrieved    
        --filter <VALUE>    my         {all|my|auto} Show all environments, only
                                       user's environments or environments      
                                       launched by any automation

commands.payload:
DESCRIPTION:
List Torque Environment

USAGE:
    torque-cli environment list [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli env list --show-ended

OPTIONS:
                            DEFAULT                                             
    -h, --help                         Prints help information                  
    -t, --token                        The user long token                      
    -p, --profile                      The user profile name                    
    -s, --space                        The space name                           
    -r, --repo                         The repository name to use with command  
        --detail                       Show detailed output in a json format    
        --show-ended                   Show ended environments                  
        --count <N>         20         Amount of environment to be retrieved    
        --filter <VALUE>    my         {all|my|auto} Show all environments, only
                                       user's environments or environments      
                                       launched by any automation

result.stdout:
DESCRIPTION:
Start Environment

USAGE:
    torque-cli environment start <BLUEPRINT-NAME> [OPTIONS]

EXAMPLES:
    torque-cli env start demo --duration=100 --name=MyDemoEnv

ARGUMENTS:
    <BLUEPRINT-NAME>    The bluerpint name to run an envrironment from

OPTIONS:
                                DEFAULT                                         
    -h, --help                             Prints help information              
    -t, --token                            The user long token                  
    -p, --profile                          The user profile name                
    -s, --space                            The space name                       
    -r, --repo                             The repository name to use with      
                                           command                              
        --detail                           Show detailed output in a json format
    -d, --duration <MINUTES>    120        The Environment will automatically   
                                           de-provision at the end of the       
                                           provided duration (minutes)          
    -n, --name <ENV-NAME>                  Provide a name for the Environment.  
                                           If not set, the name will be         
                                           generated automatically using the    
                                           source branch (or local changes) and 
                                           current time                         
    -i, --input <KEY=VALUE>                The Blueprints inputs can be provided
                                           as a key=value pair. For example: -i 
                                           key1=value1 -i key2=value2           
    -b, --branch <BRANCH>                  Run the Blueprint version from a     
                                           remote Git branch                    
    -c, --commit <COMMIT>                  Specify a specific Commit ID. This is
                                           used in order to run a environment   
                                           from a specific Blueprint historic   
                                           version. If this parameter is used,  
                                           the Branch parameter must also be    
                                           specified                            
        --timeout <MINUTES>     30         Set how long (default timeout is 30  
                                           minutes) to block and wait before    
                                           releasingcontrol back to shell       
                                           prompt. If timeout is reached before 
                                           the desired statusthe wait loop will 
                                           be interrupted                       
    -w, --wait                             Block shell prompt and wait for the  
                                           Environment to be Active while the   
                                           timeout is not reached.Default       
                                           timeout is 30 minutes.The default    
                                           timeout can be changed using the     
                                           'timeout' flag

    delete <SPACE-NAME>    Delete space
